blueLockSkibidiCodes.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Codes
blueLockSkibidiCodes.subtitle: Redeem the latest codes for free Cash used on character rolls, emotes and MVP animations.
blueLockSkibidiCodes.intro: Open the Menu at the bottom of the screen, select Codes, enter a code exactly as written, and press Redeem. Rejoin the game when a newly released code does not work, because your current server may be running an older update.

redemption.title: How to Redeem Blue Lock Skibidi Codes
steps: Launch Blue Lock: Skibidi on Roblox.
Select Menu at the bottom of the screen.
Paste a working code into the code box.
Press Redeem to claim the Cash.

troubleshooting.title: Why a Blue Lock Skibidi Code May Fail
checks: The code was entered with incorrect capitalization or spacing.
The code expired after a game update.
The current server has not received the latest update.
The code was already redeemed on the same account.

tips: Copy codes exactly, including capital letters, before pasting them into the Codes box.
Rejoin a fresh server when a new code returns no reward, since older servers may not have the update yet.
Spend code Cash on character rolls before emotes and MVP animations to grow your options first.
Check back after every update, because new Blue Lock Skibidi codes usually drop alongside new characters.

blueLockSkibidiBeginnerGuide.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Beginner Guide
blueLockSkibidiBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the main controls, finish matches for Cash, and build a useful character collection without wasting early rewards.
blueLockSkibidiBeginnerGuide.intro: Blue Lock: Skibidi is a Roblox soccer game built around character abilities, dribbling, shooting, defending and match rewards. Learn the basic controls before relying on special moves, then save Cash for character rolls that improve your available playstyles.

items.title: Learn the Core Controls
items.description: Practice movement, sliding, dribbling, passing requests and shooting before using character abilities.

items.title: Play Your Position
items.description: Stay forward when using a scoring character, support possession with passing characters, or protect the goal when playing as a goalkeeper.

items.title: Keep Possession
items.description: Use Q to dribble past pressure instead of shooting immediately. Pass or request the ball when a teammate has a clearer route to the goal.

items.title: Create a Shooting Window
items.description: Move away from defenders, aim toward an open part of the goal, and use Left Click or a shooting ability after creating space.

items.title: Use Slides Carefully
items.description: Press E to challenge an opponent carrying the ball. Avoid sliding too early, because a missed challenge leaves open space behind you.

items.title: Finish the Match
items.description: Match Cash is awarded after the game ends, so remain until the result screen instead of leaving early.

items.title: Spend Early Cash on Characters
items.description: Use free code rewards and match Cash to roll for additional characters. Build a small collection before spending heavily on emotes or MVP animations.

items.title: Store Different Roles
items.description: Keep at least one scorer, one possession-focused character and one defensive or goalkeeper option, so you can adapt to different teams.

blueLockSkibidiCharacterTierList.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Character Tier List
blueLockSkibidiCharacterTierList.subtitle: Compare the core roster by scoring ability, movement, defensive value, move quality and ease of use.
blueLockSkibidiCharacterTierList.intro: S-tier characters provide the strongest overall match impact, while A-tier options remain excellent for most teams. Lower-ranked characters can still perform well when their passing, goalkeeper or starter-friendly strengths match the player's role.

characters.name: Ronaldo
characters.strength: Combines powerful finishing with strong offensive pressure and match-winning scoring potential.

characters.name: Don Lorenzo
characters.strength: Provides elite defensive control while still creating opportunities to transition into an attack.

characters.name: Kaiser
characters.strength: A reliable finishing character built around dangerous shooting opportunities.

characters.name: Luffy
characters.strength: Uses unusual movement and offensive abilities to disrupt defenders and reach shooting positions.

characters.name: Rin
characters.strength: Offers a balanced combination of shooting power, control and offensive versatility.

characters.name: Sae
characters.strength: Excels at controlling possession and creating scoring chances through passing.

characters.name: Reo
characters.strength: A versatile option that contributes through ball movement, support play and adaptable abilities.

characters.name: Gagamaru
characters.strength: Most useful when protecting the goal and using goalkeeper dives rather than leading the attack.

characters.name: Isagi
characters.strength: A straightforward beginner option for learning positioning, shooting and basic ability timing.

blueLockSkibidiCharactersAndRarities.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Characters and Rarities
blueLockSkibidiCharactersAndRarities.subtitle: Explore the standard roster, rare attackers, legendary characters, secret crossover styles and major update additions.
blueLockSkibidiCharactersAndRarities.intro: Characters are obtained through the Shop and can be equipped from Storage after they are unlocked. Each character supplies a different set of moves, making it useful to keep scoring, passing, defensive and goalkeeper options.

items.name: Gagamaru
items.bestFor: Players who prefer defending the goal

items.name: Reo
items.signatureFocus: Ball control and adaptable support moves

items.name: Rin
items.bestFor: Players who want a balanced attacker

items.name: Barou
items.signatureFocus: King's Chop Shot and forceful finishing

items.name: Character Rolls and Storage
items.signatureFocus: Roll, store and switch unlocked characters
details: Open the Shop to roll for characters.
A standard character roll costs 250 Cash.
Unlocked characters can be selected from the Storage menu.
Keep strong or rare characters instead of replacing them immediately.
Cash can also be used for emotes and MVP animations.

blueLockSkibidiControlsAndSkillsGuide.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Controls and Skills Guide
blueLockSkibidiControlsAndSkillsGuide.subtitle: Learn every official desktop control for shooting, defending, dribbling, requesting passes, diving as goalkeeper and activating character skills.
blueLockSkibidiControlsAndSkillsGuide.intro: Blue Lock Skibidi uses a small set of universal controls alongside character-specific moves. Good timing matters more than pressing every ability immediately, so keep your strongest moves available for open shots, interceptions and one-on-one situations.

items.function: Kicks the ball toward the direction your character is facing.
items.matchTiming: Face the target before shooting. Use it near the goal to finish an attack, or inside your own box to clear the ball away from danger.

items.function: Performs a sliding defensive action that can challenge the current ball carrier.
items.matchTiming: Use it after the opponent commits to a direction. Sliding too early gives the attacker time to dribble around you.

items.function: Performs the standard dribble action when playing as an outfield player.
items.matchTiming: Activate it as a defender approaches, instead of using it while you still have several steps of open space.

items.function: Changes from dribble to goalkeeper dive while you are playing in goal.
items.matchTiming: Wait for the shooter to release the ball, then dive toward its path. Early dives leave the opposite side of the goal open.

items.function: Signals that you want a teammate to send you the ball.
items.matchTiming: Request the ball after moving into open space. Repeated requests while covered rarely create a useful passing option.

items.function: Activates the numbered abilities available to your equipped character.
items.matchTiming: Check whether a move requires possession, a nearby opponent, an airborne ball or a clear shooting lane before activating it.

blueLockSkibidiGoalkeeperGuide.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Goalkeeper Guide
blueLockSkibidiGoalkeeperGuide.subtitle: Improve your positioning, dive timing, clearances and decisions against regular shots and character abilities.
blueLockSkibidiGoalkeeperGuide.intro: Goalkeepers use Q to dive and the standard shooting control to clear or distribute the ball. The safest approach is to remain centered, follow the ball rather than every player movement, and avoid committing before the attacker begins the shot.

items.title: Start in the Center
items.instruction: Stay near the middle of the goal and adjust sideways as the ball moves. This keeps both corners reachable and prevents attackers from scoring into an unguarded side.

items.title: Follow the Ball
items.instruction: Track the ball carrier and the ball's angle instead of chasing every nearby player. Move toward the near post when the attacker approaches from a wide position.

items.title: Wait Before Diving
items.instruction: Use Q only after the shot or shooting ability begins. Diving during a dribble animation allows the attacker to wait and finish into the empty side.

items.title: Respect Character Abilities
items.instruction: Powerful character shots can arrive faster or from unusual angles. Stay centered for as long as possible and react to the ball's release rather than the opening animation.

items.title: Clear or Distribute
items.instruction: After gaining control, face an open teammate or an unoccupied side of the field before kicking. Avoid sending the ball directly toward an opponent waiting outside the box.

items.title: Leave the Goal Carefully
items.instruction: Run forward only when you can reach a loose ball before the attacker. If the opponent already controls the ball, protect the goal and force them to shoot through a smaller angle.

blueLockSkibidiChemicalReactionsAndCombo.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Chemical Reactions and Combos
blueLockSkibidiChemicalReactionsAndCombo.subtitle: Set up the verified Reo and Nagi Chemical Reaction with the correct characters, passing lane, positioning and follow-up timing.
blueLockSkibidiChemicalReactionsAndCombo.intro: The Reo and Nagi Chemical Reaction is a coordinated technique added alongside the Barou update. It requires separate teammates using Reo and Nagi, with Reo starting the sequence by sending the ball directly to Nagi.

items.name: Reo and Nagi Chemical Reaction
requirements: Reo and Nagi must be controlled by players on the same team.
The Reo player must have possession of the ball.
The Nagi player must be available in a clear receiving lane.
activation: Move Nagi into space ahead of or beside the Reo player.
Have Reo kick the ball directly toward Nagi.
Complete the reaction follow-up as the pass reaches the Nagi player.
items.bestUse: Use the reaction near the attacking third when a direct pass can move the ball beyond the final defender and create an immediate finish.
items.positioning: Reo should face Nagi before passing, while Nagi should avoid standing directly behind a defender or too close to Reo.

items.name: Central Attack Setup
requirements: Reo keeps possession until the passing lane opens.
activation: Approach through the center without forcing the first pass.
Let Nagi move into the gap between defenders.
Send the Reo pass and trigger the reaction before the defense closes the space.
items.bestUse: Effective against teams that leave space between their defenders and goalkeeper.
items.positioning: Keep Nagi several steps ahead of Reo, so the reaction advances the attack instead of happening in a crowded midfield area.

items.name: Wide-to-Center Setup
requirements: Reo carries the ball along one side of the field.
Nagi moves toward the center of the penalty area.
activation: Draw a defender toward Reo on the wing.
Wait for Nagi to enter the open central lane.
Pass inward and complete the reaction before the goalkeeper can reposition.
items.bestUse: Useful when the middle is crowded at the start of the attack but opens after defenders move toward the wing.
items.positioning: Nagi should remain inside Reo's forward view and avoid moving behind the goal or outside the passing angle.

items.name: Common Reaction Mistakes
activation: Do not attempt the sequence with two players using the same character.
Do not send the pass toward a covered Nagi player.
Do not delay the follow-up after the ball reaches Nagi.
items.bestUse: Use this checklist before attempting the reaction in a competitive match.
items.positioning: A blocked passing lane, excessive distance, or another teammate intercepting the pass can stop the intended sequence.

blueLockSkibidiUpdatesAndNewCharacters.title: Blue Lock Skibidi Updates and New Characters
blueLockSkibidiUpdatesAndNewCharacters.subtitle: Follow the newest characters, update codes, team mechanics and balance changes added to Blue Lock Skibidi.
blueLockSkibidiUpdatesAndNewCharacters.intro: Blue Lock Skibidi regularly expands its roster with Blue Lock characters and crossover-inspired styles. Recent releases have included Shidou, Ichigo, Gear 5 Luffy, Barou and the Reo and Nagi Chemical Reaction.

items.gameplayChanges: The official experience title was updated to highlight Shidou and Ichigo, expanding the available character content beyond the earlier Barou and Gear 5 releases.
The release added Gear 5 Luffy to the character lineup. Related balance changes reduced Barou's power-shot strength and stopped Barou's third move from grabbing abilities.
Barou joined the roster as an attack-focused character, while Reo and Nagi received a coordinated Chemical Reaction activated by having Reo kick the ball to a teammate using Nagi.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Blue Lock Skibidi

questions.question: What is Blue Lock Skibidi?
questions.answer: Blue Lock Skibidi is an anime-inspired Roblox soccer game where players shoot, slide, dribble, dive as goalkeeper, call for passes and activate character abilities in chaotic matches.

questions.question: Where can I play Blue Lock Skibidi?
questions.answer: Blue Lock Skibidi is available on Roblox. You can join directly through the official Roblox game page without a separate download.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Blue Lock Skibidi?
questions.answer: Open the Menu at the bottom of the screen, select Codes, paste a working code exactly as written, and press Redeem to claim your Cash reward.

questions.question: How do I unlock new characters in Blue Lock Skibidi?
questions.answer: Open the Shop and spend Cash on character rolls. Each standard roll costs 250 Cash, and unlocked characters can be equipped from the Storage menu.

questions.question: What are the controls in Blue Lock Skibidi?
questions.answer: Blue Lock Skibidi uses Left Click to shoot, E to slide, Q to dribble or dive as goalkeeper, R to request the ball, and the number keys 1 to 5 to activate character moves.

questions.question: Is Blue Lock Skibidi getting updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Blue Lock Skibidi continues to add characters and content, including recent Shidou, Ichigo, Gear 5 Luffy and Barou updates alongside the Reo and Nagi Chemical Reaction.
